reasoning: The document has very high LLM fidelity scores (composite 0.9973), but automated programmatic checks identified two major structural accessibility issues. First, header and footer elements were erroneously retained in the output HTML. Second, two images lack adequate alternative text descriptions. These major programmatic defects mandate a human review.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Mana; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence, 1 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...k ink on a white background. It appears to read "S"; p1: "Unidentified image"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=1, placeholder=1, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document uses underline formatting to emphasize the lead-in terms for bullet points in the lists on pages 3, 4, and 5 (e.g., 'Dam Safety', 'Excavation', 'Groundwater Assessments'). In the extraction JSON, these phrases are represented as plain text with no preservation of the underline formatting.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Dam Safety – There was a concern of a huge impact...
concerns.output_quote: "Dam Safety – There was a concern of a huge impact on the Lumber River if the dam were to fail."

action_items: Strip the retained header and footer text from the rendered HTML.
Update the alt text for the two affected images to ensure meaningful descriptions for screen readers.
